4 Quality Information Document 
CLIMATE_OFC_BALTIC_area_averaged_anomalies 
© EU Copernicus Marine Service – Public   
CMEMS-BAL-OMI-QUID-CLIMATE-OFC-
area-averaged-anomalies 
Issue 2.0 – November 2025 
1) INTRODUCTION 
a) Product Name  
OMI_CLIMATE_OFC_BALTIC_area_averaged_anomalies. 
b) Indicator Evaluation Method 
Ocean freshwater content anomaly (OFC) is defined here, according to Boyer et al. 
(2007), as the salinity (S) deviation from a reference period (1993-2014) and is closely 
proportional to the average salinity change from z1 = 0 m to z2 = 300 m depth:  
 
OFC =    ρ(Sref, Tref, p) / ρ(0, Tref, p ) · ( Sref - S) / Sref 
 
with a spatially varying density ρ, computed based on potential temperature, salinity 
and pressure using the TEOS10 (IOC et al., 2010). 
Integrated time series for ocean freshwater content and their deviation bars are 
calculated for the Baltic Sea.  
c) Copernicus Marine products used as input for the computation 
Product name (and Type) Documentation 
BALTICSEA_MULTIYEAR_PHY_003
_011 (model reanalysis) 
QUID: 
https://catalogue.marine.copernicus.eu/documents/
QUID/CMEMS-BAL-QUID-003-011.pdf 
PUM: 
https://catalogue.marine.copernicus.eu/documents/P
UM/CMEMS-BAL-PUM-003-011_012.pdf 
  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
5 Quality Information Document 
CLIMATE_OFC_BALTIC_area_averaged_anomalies 
© EU Copernicus Marine Service – Public   
CMEMS-BAL-OMI-QUID-CLIMATE-OFC-
area-averaged-anomalies 
Issue 2.0 – November 2025 
2) SCIENTIFIC VALIDATION  
a) Baltic Sea Ocean Freshwater Content 
Climate warming has resulted in the intensification of the global hydrological cycle but 
not necessarily on the regional scale (Pratap and Markonis, 2022). The increase of net 
precipitation over land and sea areas, decrease of ice cover, and increase of ri ver 
runoff are the main components of the global hydrological cycle that increase 
freshwater content in the ocean (Boyer et al., 2007) and decrease ocean salinity. 
The method for calculating the ocean freshwater content anomaly is based on the 
daily mean s ea water salinity fields (S) derived from the Baltic Sea reanalysis product 
BALTICSEA_MULTIYEAR_PHY_003_011. The total freshwater content is determined using 
the following formula: 
 
OFC(t) = ∭V OFC(x, y, z, t) dx dy dz  
 
The vertical integral is computed using the static cell vertical thicknesses sourced from 
the reanalysis product BALTICSEA_MULTIYEAR_PHY_003_011 dataset 
cmems_mod_bal_phy_my_static, spanning from the sea surface to the ocean bottom. 
Spatial integration is performed over the Baltic Sea spatial doma

catalog_entry.summary: This Quality Information Document describes the evaluation method for the OMI_CLIMATE_OFC_BALTIC_area_averaged_anomalies product, focusing on ocean freshwater content anomalies in the Baltic Sea using Copernicus Marine Service data.
